draft:
Dear Sir or Madam:

I am writing to provide my strongest recommendation for Mr. Arjun Varma, an individual of extraordinary ability in the field of sustainability commercialization. Mr. Varma is a rare talent whose expertise and contributions have had a profound impact on the sustainability landscape. His unique ability to bridge the gap between innovative sustainable technologies and their practical, commercial applications sets him apart as a true leader in his field.

As for my background, I currently serve as a Climate Counselor at the U.S. Department of the Treasury, where I have been since March 2023. In this role, I advise on climate-related financial policies and risks, leveraging my extensive experience in risk management and financial services. Prior to this, I had a long and distinguished career at Zenith Bank, where I held various roles from 1982 to 2020. During my tenure, I served as the Chief Risk Officer, overseeing the company's risk management frameworks and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. My expertise in risk management was a critical component of my work at Zenith Bank, and it continues to inform my contributions at the Treasury.

My educational background includes a Bachelor's degree in Economics from Harvard University, which I obtained in 1982. This foundation in economics has been instrumental in my understanding of financial systems and risk management. Throughout my career, I have developed a strong skill set in risk management, which has been a cornerstone of my professional accomplishments. My experience at Zenith Bank and my current role at the U.S. Department of the Treasury have provided me with a deep understanding of financial systems, risk management, and the intersection of finance and climate policy.

I had the pleasure of working closely with Mr. Varma during his secondment to Green Horizon Partners, where he served as Chief of Staff to the COO. Green Horizon Partners is a $1.5 billion investment vehicle dedicated to reducing tropical deforestation by working directly with country governments. As a small team of only 30 people, each member plays a crucial role in driving the organization's mission forward. In his role as Chief of Staff, Mr. Varma was instrumental in supporting the COO and ensuring the smooth operation of Green Horizon's initiatives.

During his tenure at Green Horizon Partners, Mr. Varma demonstrated remarkable leadership and analytical skills. He was pivotal in revamping the carbon credit pricing mechanism for a $1.5 billion fund aimed at stopping tropical deforestation. By transitioning from a static to a dynamic pricing model, he significantly enhanced the fund's effectiveness and impact. Additionally, Mr. Varma co-led the annual board meeting, collaborating with esteemed figures such as the former Chief Risk Officer of Zenith Bank and the Managing Director of the Global Environmental League. His ability to engage with high-level stakeholders and drive strategic discussions was truly impressive.

Mr. Varma's contributions to Green Horizon Partners were not only impactful but also indicative of his broader capabilities in the field of sustainability. His work on the carbon credit pricing mechanism showcased his deep understanding of financial instruments and their application to environmental challenges. By implementing a dynamic pricing model, he ensured that the fund could adapt to changing market conditions and maximize its impact on deforestation reduction. This innovative approach demonstrated his ability to think strategically and develop solutions that address complex, real-world problems.

Moreover, Mr. Varma's leadership in co-leading the annual board meeting highlighted his exceptional communication and collaboration skills. Engaging with senior leaders from both the financial and environmental sectors, he facilitated critical discussions that shaped the organization's strategic direction. His ability to bridge the gap between diverse stakeholders and drive consensus was invaluable to Green Horizon Partners's mission.

Beyond his specific achievements at Green Horizon Partners, Mr. Varma's work exemplifies his broader expertise in sustainability commercialization. His ability to develop and implement innovative financial mechanisms that drive environmental impact is a testament to his unique skill set. By translating complex sustainability concepts into actionable strategies, he has made significant contributions to the field and advanced the mission of organizations like Green Horizon Partners.

In addition to his remarkable contributions at Green Horizon Partners, Mr. Varma has demonstrated extraordinary ability in the field of sustainability commercialization through his work at BCG & Associates. During his tenure as a Senior Business Analyst and Sustainability Fellow, he delivered outstanding results on numerous high-impact projects, further solidifying his reputation as a leader in sustainability.

One of Mr. Varma's standout achievements at BCG was his work with a sustainable cement startup. He developed an EU expansion strategy that helped the company raise over $100 million in equity, unlocking significant growth opportunities. This project not only showcased his strategic thinking and analytical skills but also underscored his ability to drive tangible financial outcomes for sustainable businesses.

Mr. Varma also played a crucial role in crafting an investment thesis for a $200 million private equity carveout dedicated to solar energy. He identified 10 high-priority targets and successfully conducted due diligence on the first two investments. His work in this area demonstrated his deep understanding of the renewable energy sector and his ability to identify and evaluate promising investment opportunities.

Another notable project involved building a financial model and impact analysis for a $2 billion fundraising initiative to launch a new renewable energy nonprofit organization at COP26. Mr. Varma's expertise in financial modeling and impact assessment was instrumental in securing the necessary funding and laying the groundwork for the organization's future success.

Furthermore, Mr. Varma developed a strategy and roadmap for a global agriculture player to launch new business segments in digital agriculture, alternative proteins, and sustainable fertilizer. His efforts led to the allocation of approximately $50 million to pilot these business units, positioning them as potential growth sectors. This project highlighted his ability to drive innovation and create value in the agriculture industry.

In addition to his project work, Mr. Varma was selected as one of only two Sustainability Fellows in BCG's New York office, a testament to his expertise and leadership in sustainability. This highly competitive fellowship recognized his ability to deliver exceptional results and his commitment to advancing BCG's sustainability initiatives.

Mr. Varma's work at BCG further exemplifies his extraordinary ability in sustainability commercialization. His strategic insights, analytical prowess, and commitment to driving positive environmental impact have made him an invaluable asset to the firm and the clients he served. His contributions have not only advanced BCG's sustainability agenda but have also set a new standard for excellence in the field.

Beyond his professional achievements, Mr. Varma's educational background and entrepreneurial ventures further underscore his extraordinary ability in sustainability commercialization. He holds a Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering with a Minor in Economics from Stanford University, where he was recognized with several honors, including the Undergraduate Computing Prize and multiple appearances on the Dean's List. His academic foundation in engineering and economics has equipped him with the technical and analytical skills necessary to excel in his field.

While at Stanford, Mr. Varma also engaged in research on geospatial machine learning models to measure electricity access and solar geo-engineering policy frameworks. This research experience has provided him with a deep understanding of the technical and policy aspects of sustainability, further enhancing his ability to develop innovative solutions to complex environmental challenges.

In addition to his academic pursuits, Mr. Varma has demonstrated his entrepreneurial spirit and commitment to sustainability through various ventures. He co-founded FreshHold, a cold storage startup in India focused on food security and vaccine delivery. Under his leadership, the company raised $25,000 to build and pilot a cold storage and distribution system that reduced food waste by 30% at 40% lower cost. FreshHold's innovative approach to addressing food security challenges earned it recognition as a Hult Prize global finalist, placing it among the top 15 teams out of 100,000, and as a Resolution Prize winner at the Clinton Global Initiative.

Mr. Varma's entrepreneurial journey continued with the founding of FiberLink, a company that converts agricultural waste into sustainable feedstocks for energy and materials. As a Co-founder, he has led the development of a plasma reactor to extract cellulose from waste with 80% lower emissions and 30% lower cost compared to the status quo. FiberLink has secured significant funding and established key partnerships, further demonstrating Mr. Varma's ability to drive innovation and create value in the sustainability sector.

In conclusion, Mr. Arjun Varma is an individual of extraordinary ability in the field of sustainability commercialization. His impressive track record at BCG & Associates, coupled with his entrepreneurial ventures and strong academic background, underscore his unique talent and commitment to driving positive environmental impact. Mr. Varma's ability to develop and implement innovative solutions, his strategic insights, and his dedication to advancing sustainability make him an invaluable asset to any organization. His contributions have not only advanced the missions of the companies he has worked with but have also set a new standard for excellence in the field of sustainability. It is without reservation that I provide my strongest recommendation for Mr. Varma.

Yours truly,

Howard WellingtonClimate CounselorU.S. Department of the Treasury[email contact here]
final:
Dear Sir or Madam:

It is with utmost confidence that I recommend Mr. Arjun Varma, a highly skilled individual recognized for his extraordinary competence in the area of sustainability commercialization. Mr. Varma is a rare talent whose expertise and contributions have had a profound impact on the sustainability landscape. His unique ability to bridge the gap between innovative sustainable technologies and their practical, commercial applications sets him apart as a true leader in his field.

As for my background, I currently serve as a Climate Counselor at the U.S. Department of the Treasury, where I have been since March 2023. In this role, I advise on climate-related financial policies and risks, leveraging my extensive experience in risk management and financial services. Prior to this, I had a long and distinguished career at Zenith Bank, where I held various roles from 1982 to 2020. During my tenure, I served as the Chief Risk Officer, overseeing the company's risk management frameworks and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. My expertise in risk management was a critical component of my work at Zenith Bank, and it continues to inform my contributions at the Treasury. This deep understanding of financial systems, risk management, and the intersection of finance and climate policy provides me with the necessary perspective to evaluate talents like Mr. Varma.

I had the privilege of working with Mr. Varma during his period at Green Horizon Partners, where he effectively served as Chief of Staff to the COO. Green Horizon Partners is a $1.5 billion investment platform aimed at reducing tropical deforestation through direct engagement with national governments. As part of our compact team of 30, Mr. Varma played an essential role in advancing the organization’s objectives, providing critical support to the COO, and ensuring efficient operations. His contributions here laid the foundation for his later successes in the sustainability sector.

Throughout his time at Green Horizon Partners, Mr. Varma displayed remarkable leadership and analytical capabilities. He spearheaded the overhaul of the carbon credit pricing strategy, vital for a substantial fund dedicated to halting deforestation. By shifting to a dynamic pricing model, he greatly enhanced the fund's efficacy and impact. Moreover, Mr. Varma led the annual board meeting, interacting with distinguished leaders such as the former Chief Risk Officer of Zenith Bank and the Managing Director of the Global Environmental League. His proficiency in engaging with senior stakeholders and steering strategic discussions was notable, showcasing his readiness for larger challenges.

Building on his achievements at Green Horizon Partners, Mr. Varma demonstrated his extraordinary abilities in sustainability commercialization during his tenure at BCG & Associates. As a Senior Business Analyst, he delivered outstanding results on numerous high-impact projects, which further solidified his reputation as a sustainability leader. His work with a sustainable cement startup, where he developed an EU expansion strategy, is a prime example. This strategy helped the company raise over $100 million in equity, showcasing his capability to drive financial success in sustainable businesses.

Continuing to excel, Mr. Varma played a crucial role in crafting an investment thesis for a $200 million private equity carveout dedicated to solar energy. He identified high-priority investment targets and conducted successful due diligence, demonstrating a deep understanding of the renewable energy sector. These projects at BCG highlighted his strategic thinking and capability to innovate within the sustainability domain.

In addition to his remarkable project work, Mr. Varma achieved the distinguished honor of being selected as one of only two Sustainability Fellows in BCG's New York office. This prestigious and highly competitive fellowship underscores his profound expertise and leadership in the field of sustainability. As a Sustainability Fellow, he was tasked with developing innovative solutions to complex environmental challenges. His role involved collaborating with cross-functional teams to integrate sustainability principles into client projects, demonstrating both his technical acumen and his commitment to advancing BCG's sustainability initiatives.

Mr. Varma has also passionately demonstrated his entrepreneurial spirit through several ventures. He founded FreshHold, a cold storage startup in India focusing on enhancing food security and vaccine delivery. This venture earned international accolades, underscoring his ability to innovate and address critical global challenges.

Further cementing his entrepreneurial prowess, Mr. Varma founded FiberLink, a company transforming agricultural waste into sustainable feedstocks. As a founder and commercial lead, he developed a groundbreaking plasma reactor, reducing emissions and costs significantly. His leadership at FiberLink exemplifies his capacity to drive tangible value and position companies at the forefront of sustainable technology.

Beyond his professional contributions, Mr. Varma has been selected for prestigious memberships, each marked by their rigorous selection processes, underscoring his exceptional commitment and impact in the field of sustainability. As a part of the Entrepreneur First Accelerator, he joined an elite group of entrepreneurial talents, chosen through an extremely competitive process designed to identify individuals capable of launching groundbreaking ventures and fostering innovative solutions. Similarly, his inclusion in the On Deck Climate Technology Fellowship underlines his deep dedication to tackling climate issues. This fellowship is known for bringing together the brightest minds in climate tech, selecting those with the potential to drive substantial environmental change. Furthermore, Mr. Varma's role in the Harvard Business Review Council allows him to participate in shaping high-level discussions on sustainability and business strategy, a privilege reserved for those with influential insights and strategic acumen. His membership in the Voyagers Climate Family further reflects his place in a selectively curated community of leaders and innovators, all committed to advancing ambitious global environmental goals. These highly selective memberships confirm Mr. Varma's exceptional abilities and forward-thinking vision, while also affirming his steadfast dedication to making a lasting, meaningful impact in sustainability. Through these distinguished platforms, he continues to be a key influencer in the evolution of sustainable practices and technologies.

In essence, Mr. Arjun Varma's impressive array of achievements underscores his extraordinary ability in sustainability commercialization. His work at BCG, entrepreneurial ventures, and participation in elite programs illustrate a career defined by innovation, strategic thinking, and a deep commitment to environmental impact. Mr. Varma stands out not only for his technical acumen and leadership skills but also for his genuine dedication to advancing sustainable solutions on a global scale. His contributions have set new standards for excellence in the field, and his ongoing endeavors promise to further enhance his impact on the sustainability landscape.

Please feel welcome to reach out for any further details or explanations you might require.

Very truly yours,

Howard WellingtonClimate CounselorU.S. Department of the Treasury[Email contact here]
